item.title: Johnson & Johnson Immunology & Q1 2026 Earnings
item.description:
• Summary: Johnson & Johnson reported strong Q1 2026 earnings, driven by its immunology portfolio, particularly Tremfya (guselkumab) and the newly launched Icotyde. The company is increasingly confident in managing the upcoming loss of exclusivity for Stelara (ustekinumab), targeting $100 billion in revenue by 2026.
  • MoAs: Tremfya (guselkumab): interleukin-23 (IL-23) inhibitor; Stelara (ustekinumab): interleukin-12/23 (IL-12/23) inhibitor.
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma, BioPharma Dive, BioSpace, Endpoints News

item.title: Astellas Closes Seattle Site / Harbinger
item.description:
• Summary: Astellas Pharma is closing its Universal Cells stem cell unit's Seattle office, impacting 50 employees, as part of a restructuring move. Concurrently, Harbinger has raised $100 million in funding.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioSpace, Endpoints News, Fierce Biotech

item.title: Eli Lilly / CrossBridge ADC Acquisition
item.description:
• Summary: Eli Lilly has agreed to acquire CrossBridge Bio for up to $300 million to bolster its antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) pipeline for cancer treatments. This deal aims to bridge gaps in cancer care with novel ADC therapies.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, BioSpace, Endpoints News

item.title: Corporate Deals & Financings
item.description:
• Summary: A wave of corporate deals and financings is sweeping the biopharma industry, including Obsidian Therapeutics' reverse merger with Galera to go public, Revolution Medicines leading stock offerings totaling $1.5 billion, and Kailera eyeing a record $533 million IPO for obesity drugs.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech, Endpoints News, BioSpace

item.title: Q1 2026 Pharma Earnings Season
item.description:
• Summary: The pharmaceutical industry enters the Q1 2026 earnings season with a focus on financial stability and deal-making, as companies report results amid ongoing market dynamics and strategic partnerships.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioSpace

item.title: Australia's Kazia Epigenetic Platform Deal
item.description:
• Summary: Kazia Therapeutics has acquired an epigenetic platform from a research institute for $14 million to enhance its preclinical drug development pipeline, potentially in oncology or other therapeutic areas.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: FierceBiotech
